title: Ethics Protocol and Data Management Plan. Long-Term Ethnographic Research with People Experiencing Houselessness in Leipzig (2018–2022)
author: Schneider, Luisa
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This document presents the full ethics protocol and data management plan for a long-term ethnographic study conducted with people experiencing houselessness in Leipzig, Germany (2018–2022). The protocol was originally developed in 2018 to guide fieldwork, reviewed and refined bi-annually during the research process, and consolidated into this comprehensive archival version (v1.0) in 2025. 
 The protocol includes: – participant information sheets (plain language, German and English) – written, oral, and processual consent frameworks – a GDPR-compliant data management plan – data flow diagrams – anonymisation and data protection strategies – researcher positionality statement – relational and trauma-informed ethics framework – risk assessment (participant, researcher, community) – detailed fieldwork procedures (step-by-step) – long-term data retention and deletion plan – version history documenting ethical review across the project lifecycle 
 This consolidated version reflects the ethical practice as it was developed, applied, and refined throughout years of immersive, relational fieldwork, and subsequent analysis, publication, and long-term archiving processes. It is intended to support transparency, scholarly accountability, and methodological clarity for future research, ethics reviews, and publications.
